spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/src/main/java.general_idea: Replace field injection with constructor injection for Spring beans to adhere to modern best practices and support final fields.
Rename logger field to uppercase constant to follow Java naming conventions for static/final fields.
Update import statements to reflect the new repository class names and remove unused autowired import.
Add demonstration code for the new JDBC Client approach alongside the existing JDBC Template approach.
Add logging headers to distinguish between JDBC Template and JDBC Client execution blocks.
Add a new method to serialize the Student object to a JSON string using Java 21 text blocks.
spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/src/main/java.after: public String toJSON() { return """ { "id" : "%s", "name" : "%s", "passportNumber" : "%s", } """.formatted(id, name, passportNumber); }

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.general_idea: Remove unused SLF4J logger import and field declaration.
Reformat indentation from tabs to spaces.
Replace manual boilerplate (constructors, getters, setters, toString) with Lombok annotations.

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.general_idea: Update indentation style from tabs to spaces.

spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.general_idea: Remove explicit @Autowired annotation in favor of constructor injection for the StudentRepository dependency.
Rename logger field from camelCase 'logger' to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE 'LOGGER' to follow Java naming conventions for constants.
Update all logger method calls to use the new field name 'LOGGER'.
Remove the throws Exception clause from the run method signature as it is no longer required.

spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.general_idea: Move the main method to the end of the class for better code organization.
Add a final log statement to print all users after the initial operations.
Replace manual boilerplate methods (getters, setters, constructors, toString) with Lombok annotations to reduce code verbosity.

spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.general_idea: Remove the explicit no-argument constructor since it is now provided by the @NoArgsConstructor Lombok annotation.
Remove the explicit all-arguments constructor since it is now provided by the @AllArgsConstructor Lombok annotation.
spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.before: public Student(Long id, String name, String passportNumber) { ... }

spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.general_idea: Remove the explicit two-argument constructor as it is no longer strictly required for JPA (which uses the no-arg constructor) and simplifies the class structure.
Remove the explicit toString() method since it is now generated by the @Data Lombok annotation.
Remove the comment indicating the migration from javax to jakarta, as the migration is now complete and the comment is obsolete.

spring-boot-2-myBatis-with-h2/src/main/j.general_idea: Migrate from field-based dependency injection to constructor-based injection to align with modern Spring Boot best practices.
Rename logger field to uppercase constant and mark as final to follow Java naming conventions for static/final fields.
Remove unused import for @Autowired annotation as field injection is replaced by constructor injection.
Remove 'throws Exception' from CommandLineRunner.run method signature as it is no longer required or used.

spring-boot-2-myBatis-with-h2/src/main/j.general_idea: Refactor the Student class from a traditional POJO with manual getters/setters to a Java record for conciseness.
spring-boot-2-myBatis-with-h2/src/main/j.before: public class Student { private Long id; private String name; private String passport; public Student() { super(); } public Student(Long id, String name, String passport) { super(); this.id = id; this.name = name; this.passport = passport; } public Student(String name, String passport) { super(); this.name = name; this.passport = passport; } public Long getId() { return id; } public void setId(Long id) { this.id = id; } public String getName() { return name; } public void setName(String name) { this.name = name; } public String getPassport() { return passport; } public void setPassport(String passport) { this.passport = passport; } @Override public String toString() { return String.format("Student [id=%s, name=%s, passport=%s]", id, name, passport); } }
spring-boot-2-myBatis-with-h2/src/main/j.after: public record Student(Long id, String name, String passport) { }
